lint.command: go test ./apps/backend/internal/tracking/... -count=1 -parallel 4 && go test ./apps/backend/internal/bootstrap/... -count=1 -parallel 4 && vp run test:e2e:website -- e2e/timer-page.spec.ts --workers 1

blockingIssues.description: The backend-truth proof now exists, but the feature still violates the mission traceability contract because `docs/research/toggl-timer-view-family.md` and `docs/research/toggl-timer-top-entry-form.md` each contain two active mission status blocks instead of exactly one.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Keep or strengthen the explicit end-of-run check that the handoff commit itself contains the files and behavior claimed in the worker handoff before EndFeatureRun succeeds.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The original failed feature `global-running-timer-contract-and-regressions` reported commit `54b030b` even though that commit did not include the claimed docs/regression work; the repair review passed only after the handoff was corrected to an auditable commit and the repo retained `.factory` state.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen enforcement that browser-visible timer features must include the required agent-browser replay and must not mark `followedProcedure: true` when that replay is absent.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The original `timer-core-shared-page-orchestration-refactor` handoff marked procedure-followed even though no `agent-browser` replay or interactive checks were recorded; the missing replay only appeared in the later follow-up session that landed commit `45e0674`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit duplicate-block warning and example that workers must update the existing active mission status block in a source doc rather than appending a second block.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The still-failing feature `timer-core-cross-workspace-header-backend-proof-and-traceability` appended a second active block in both directly used docs despite the existing one-block rule, showing the current wording is easy to misapply.
